result.summary: Found 5 issues on the page, primarily related to content being clipped at the right edge without clear scroll affordance indicators, and truncated text in navigation.

issues.description: The navigation category tab 'Hot Gam' is visibly cut off at the right edge, making the label unreadable. Users cannot see the full name of the category.
issues.suggestion: Ensure the horizontal scroll container for navigation tabs shows a visible scroll indicator or fades the edge to signal scrollability, and avoid hard-clipping text mid-word.

issues.description: Multiple game card titles on the right side of each row are abruptly clipped (e.g., 'MAGNI PHO...', 'MUNC MI...', 'HYPERC...', 'DAR WATE THE CURSE'), giving the appearance of broken or missing content rather than a scrollable carousel.
issues.suggestion: Add a visual fade-out gradient on the right edge of each game row to clearly communicate that content is horizontally scrollable, or add visible navigation arrows like those used in the banner carousel.

issues.description: The horizontally scrollable game rows lack visible scroll affordance (no arrows, no scroll indicator), which may confuse users—especially those on non-touch devices—into thinking the layout is broken.
issues.suggestion: Add right-side arrow buttons or a horizontal scrollbar to the game rows so users on desktop clearly understand the rows are scrollable.

issues.description: The 'BetRivers Exclusive Games' section shows 6 game tiles but the rightmost ones are partially hidden. Users may not realize there are 54 games available beyond what's visible.
issues.suggestion: Use a '54 Games' button that is more visually prominent, or add a 'See All' call-to-action within the row itself to guide users to explore more games.

issues.description: The bottom navigation bar icon for 'Casino' appears to use a cherry/slot icon which is inconsistent in style compared to the other icons (cards, basketball, poker chip, megaphone, headset).
issues.suggestion: Ensure all bottom navigation icons follow a consistent visual style and weight for a more polished look.
